description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with light brown shingles and prominent white trim along the edges. The exterior walls are clad in blue siding with a brick foundation and a large, brick chimney rising along one side. The front facade includes a covered porch supported by square columns, a set of steps leading up to the entryway, and a bay window that projects outward. Multiple windows of varying sizes are symmetrically and asymmetrically arranged, including a distinctive bay window and a small attic window above the porch. The rear of the house is simpler, with a single door and two windows, while the side elevations reveal additional bay and standard windows. The overall design suggests a Craftsman or early 20th-century American suburban style, with a focus on functional, homey details and a welcoming front porch.
description: A blue-sided house with a steep gable roof, brick chimney, covered front porch, and bay windows, featuring white trim and a brick foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, central steps, bay window, and main entryway beneath the gable roof.
description_view_1: Left-front angle highlighting the tall brick chimney, side bay window, and the intersection of rooflines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a simple facade with a single door and two windows, and the broad expanse of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Right-front angle showing the side bay window, porch, and the continuation of the roof and chimney.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with bay windows and standard rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, square columns, overhanging gable roof, and a central door
